content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 18, 1994

Michael B. Montgomery
13200 Crossroads Parkway North
Suite 350
City of Industry, CA 91746

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-93-469

Dear Mr. Montgomery:

This is in response to your letter requesting advice on
behalf of Eileen Ansari, Diamond Bar City Councilmember, regarding
her responsibilities under the conflict-of-interest provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ This letter also shall
serve to confirm the oral advice we gave you on December 8, 1993.

QUESTIONS

1. Under the conflict-of interest provisions of the Act,
should Councilmember Ansari disqualify herself from voting to
retain you as interim city attorney, because of your continued
representation of her against private defendants who have no
relationship to the city?

2. May Councilmember Ansari participate in city council
decisions regarding your pay warrants?

CONCLUSIONS

1. This question refers to conduct of Councilmember Ansari
in connection with a decision that has already occurred, i.e.,
your retention as interim city attorney. Regulation
18329(b)(8)(A) provides that formal written advice must be
declined where the "requestor is seeking advice relating to past
conduct." Thus, we cannot provide you with advice concerning that
conduct.

2. This question is prospective in nature and for that
reason we are able to provide advice. From the facts you have
provided, it would appear that you are not a source of income to

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-93-469
Page 2


Councilmember Ansari. (In fact, she is a source of income to
you.) Thus, under the Act, you are not an economic interest of
hers and she would not have a financial interest in a decision
because of you. Consequently, unless city council decisions
concerning your pay warrants will have a material financial effect
on Councilmember Ansari directly or on any economic interest of
hers, she may participate in such decisions.

FACTS

Six years ago, you filed a lawsuit on behalf of Aziz and
Eileen Ansari against the sellers and developers of their
residence for damages that resulted from improperly compacted
fill. The initial attorney retainer agreement was on a per diem
basis. However, the pleadings, paper work, and appearances became
so extensive and time consuming that you modified the fee contract
to a contingency arrangement. Since that time, the Ansaris have
paid for only out-of-pocket costs, such as depositions, witness
fees, etc.

On the eve of trial, the trial court granted judgment on the
pleadings in favor of the major defendant, based upon the
defendant's contention that the statute of limitations barred
recovery, i.e., more than three years had transpired prior to the
filing of a lawsuit since the predecessor in interest presumably
had notice of the improper soil conditions. You filed a notice of
appeal with the Second District Court of Appeal. Though all
briefing has been completed, oral argument has yet to be scheduled
by the Court of Appeal. If the trial court's judgment in favor of
the major defendant is sustained by the Court of Appeal, your
clients, the Ansaris, will nevertheless seek recovery from the
other judgment defendants. If the trial court judgment is
reversed, the matter will be remitted back to the superior court
for trial.

On November 9, 1993, Eileen Ansari was elected to the Diamond
Bar City Council and was sworn in on December 7, 1993. At that
meeting, the city council terminated the services of its city
attorney. In a subsequent motion, the city council voted in a
split decision (3-2) to retain you as the interim city attorney.
Councilmember Ansari voted with the majority over strong
opposition from the two dissenting councilmembers. Therefore, you
stated that Councilmember Ansari's vote was necessary for your
retention and will presumably be necessary to approve city
attorney warrant registers in the future.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making,
participating in making, or otherwise using his or her official
position to influence a governmental decision in which the
official has a financial interest. Section 87103 specifies that
an official has a financial interest if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial

Our File No. A-93-469
Page 3


effect, distinguishable from the effect on the public generally,
on the official or a member of his or her immediate family or on:

     (a)  Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

     (b)  Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

     (c)  Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.

     (d)  Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

     (e)  Any donor of, or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by, or promised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made.

                              Section 87103.

     From the facts you have provided, Councilmember Ansari has
not received any income from you.  Thus, under the Act, you are
not a "source of income" to her, i.e., you do not have a financial interest in a decision
because of you.

     Consequently, unless city council decisions concerning your
pay warrants will have a material financial effect on
Councilmember Ansari directly or on any economic interest of hers
(as set forth in Section 87103 above), she may participate in such
decisions.




_____________________________
2  Rather, it is just the reverse.  You have received income from
Councilmember Ansari and she is an economic interest of yours.

Our File No. A-93-469
Page 4


If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.3

                                                    Sincerely,

                                                    Steven G. Churchwell
                                                    General Counsel

                                                    [signature]
                                                    By: Deanne Stone
                                                    Senior Commission Counsel




3    Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.  Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.
